```{r process-issue}
        # Merge with any existing markers (existing take precedence)
        if (nrow(page_index) > 0) {
          synth_df <- synth_df[!synth_df$page %in% page_index$page, ]
        }
        page_index <- rbind(page_index, synth_df)
        page_index <- page_index[order(page_index$page), ]
        cat(sprintf("  Synthetic page index: %d total markers (interpolated from CONTENTS pages %d-%d)\n",
                    nrow(page_index), pg_min, pg_max))
      }
    }
  }

  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  # Step 5: TWO-PASS HYBRID SEARCH
  #
  # PASS A — Independent search (no sequential advancement):
  #   Each article searches its own page-windowed region of the full body,
  #   independently of every other article. search_from is NOT advanced.
  #   This means Strategy 10/11 false positives cannot cascade: if article [i]
  #   gets a wrong position, it cannot push article [i+1]'s search forward.
  #
  # PASS B — Order validation:
  #   The CONTENTS table defines the true article order. After all candidates
  #   are found, enforce monotonicity: if candidate[i] <= candidate[i-1]
  #   (i.e., this article appears to start before or at the same place as the
  #   previous one), the candidate is a false match and is discarded.
  #   The article then falls through to the page-estimate fallback in Step 6.
  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  candidates <- rep(NA_integer_, nrow(arts))   # raw finds from Pass A
  positions  <- rep(NA_integer_, nrow(arts))   # validated positions from Pass B
  CPP <- 2400L

  # ---- Pass A: independent per-article search ----
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(arts))) {
    title <- arts$title[i]
    pg    <- arts$page[i]
    cat_i <- arts$category[i]

    # Frontispiece / page-0 articles: always near start of body
    if (cat_i == "Frontispiece" || pg <= 0L) {
      ft <- str_trim(str_replace(title, regex("\\s+Frontispiece\\s*$",
                                              ignore_case=TRUE), ""))
      win_end <- min(body_end, body_start + 6000L)
      pos <- if (nchar(ft) >= 3)
        find_in_window(text, ft, body_start, win_end)
      else NA_integer_
      if (is.na(pos))
        pos <- find_in_window(text, title, body_start, win_end)
      if (!is.na(pos) &&
          !(cat_i == "Frontispiece" && (pos - body_start) > 2000L)) {
        # Distance guard: frontispiece captions live near body_start.
        # A match >2000 chars away is a false positive in article body.
        candidates[i] <- max(snap_to_para_start(text, pos, body_start, max_back=300L), body_start)
      } else if (cat_i == "Frontispiece") {
        # Image-only frontispiece OR distant false positive: leave as NA.
        # Step 11 will search pre-body regions; otherwise logged as NIS.
        # Do NOT fall back to body_start — that merges with the first
        # real article and absorbs its content.
      } else {
        candidates[i] <- body_start   # page-0 non-frontispiece fallback
      }
      next
    }

    # Normal articles: narrow → wide → full-body, each bounded by page estimate.
    # IMPORTANT: search always starts from body_start (not search_from),
    # so articles can be found even if a previous article's position was wrong.
    if (nrow(page_index) >= 3) {
      pg_est <- page_to_pos(page_index, pg, body_start, body_end)
      # Only use narrow/wide windows when page index has enough markers (>= 6)
      # for reliable position estimates. With fewer markers, interpolation is too
      # coarse and the window may center on the wrong occurrence of a title
      # (e.g. in lesson previews instead of the actual article).
      pos <- NA_integer_
      if (nrow(page_index) >= 6) {
        # Narrow window: pg ± 3–4 pages
        s1 <- max(body_start,
                  page_to_pos(page_index, max(1L, pg-3L), body_start, body_end) - CPP)
        e1 <- min(body_end,
                  page_to_pos(page_index, pg+4L, body_start, body_end) + CPP)
        pos <- find_in_window(text, title, s1, e1, allow_s10=TRUE)
        # Wide window: pg ± 6–7 pages
        if (is.na(pos)) {
          s2 <- max(body_start,
                    page_to_pos(page_index, max(1L, pg-6L), body_start, body_end) - CPP*2L)
          e2 <- min(body_end,
                    page_to_pos(page_index, pg+7L, body_start, body_end) + CPP*2L)
          pos <- find_in_window(text, title, s2, e2, allow_s10=TRUE)
        }
      }
      # Full-body fallback: plausibility-checked against pg_est.
      # Conference proceedings articles (Work and Business Meeting, General Session,
      # etc.) often appear as subsection headings EARLIER in the body than their
      # page number suggests — use a wider tolerance (50,000 chars) for those.
      # SPECIAL CASE: When pg_est was clamped to body_end (because ALL page markers
      # extrapolate beyond body_end), the pg_est is unreliable — it simply reflects
      # the end of the file, not a real position estimate. For conference session
      # headings in this situation the gap can exceed 100k chars (e.g. Jun16 where
      # "Work and Business Meeting" is at @25816 but pg_est = body_end = 149966).
      # In that case, skip the plausibility check entirely and use the full-body find.
      if (is.na(pos)) {
        pos_fb <- find_in_window(text, title, body_start, body_end, allow_s10=FALSE)
        if (!is.na(pos_fb)) {
          # pg_est is unreliable when:
          #   (a) clamped to body_end  — page number extrapolates beyond the file, OR
          #   (b) extrapolated below body_start — inverted/disordered page markers
          # In either case the estimate is meaningless, so skip the plausibility check.
          # pg_est is unreliable when it falls outside the body:
          #   >= body_end : extrapolation put the estimate past the end of the file
          #                 (covers both clamped-to-body_end AND unclamped overshoots,
          #                  e.g. pg352 in a conference issue where the last page marker
          #                  is pg338 → estimate = 169,222 >> body_end = 149,966)
          #   < body_start: negative/backward extrapolation from inverted page markers
          pg_est_is_clamped  <- !is.na(pg_est) && (pg_est >= body_end)
          pg_est_is_negative <- !is.na(pg_est) && (pg_est < body_start)
          pg_est_unreliable  <- pg_est_is_clamped || pg_est_is_negative
          plausible <- is.na(pg_est) ||
                       pg_est_unreliable  ||
                       abs(pos_fb - pg_est) <= 25000L
          pos <- if (plausible) pos_fb else NA_integer_
        }
      }
      # S10 full-body fallback: uses author-proximity strategies (allow_s10=TRUE).
      if (is.na(pos)) {
        pos_fb2 <- find_in_window(text, title, body_start, body_end, allow_s10=TRUE)
        if (!is.na(pos_fb2)) {
          pg_est_is_clamped2  <- !is.na(pg_est) && (pg_est >= body_end)
          pg_est_is_negative2 <- !is.na(pg_est) && (pg_est < body_start)
          pg_est_unreliable2  <- pg_est_is_clamped2 || pg_est_is_negative2
          plausible2 <- is.na(pg_est) ||
                        pg_est_unreliable2 ||
                        abs(pos_fb2 - pg_est) <= 25000L
          pos <- if (plausible2) pos_fb2 else NA_integer_
        }
      }
    } else {
      # No page index: search full body with S10/S11.
      pos <- find_in_window(text, title, body_start, body_end, allow_s10=TRUE)
    }

    if (!is.na(pos)) {
      candidates[i] <- max(snap_to_para_start(text, pos, body_start), body_start)
    }
  }

  # ---- Pass B: Enforce monotonic ordering ----
  # Articles are already in page order from parse_contents_section().
  # Enforce that positions increase monotonically: each article's position
  # must be >= the previous article's accepted position.  This ensures the
  # script processes the file from beginning to end without splicing.
  #
  # If a candidate position is BEFORE the previous article's position,
  # the match is likely a false hit (e.g. a repeated word earlier in the
  # file).  Re-search the title ONLY in the valid forward region.
  min_allowed_pos <- body_start
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(arts))) {
    if (!is.na(candidates[i])) {
      if (candidates[i] >= min_allowed_pos) {
        # Candidate is in valid forward position — accept it
        positions[i] <- candidates[i]
        # "Notes from/to the Field" are catch-all sections whose positions
        # can be unreliable (e.g., matching a generic header ahead of where
        # the section actually begins).  Don't let them advance
        # min_allowed_pos — this prevents cascading displacement of
        # subsequent articles (editorial, lessons) that may appear earlier
        # in the body text despite having higher page numbers.
        is_notes <- str_detect(arts$title[i],
                               regex("^Notes\\s+(from|to)\\s+the\\s+Field",
                                     ignore_case=TRUE))
        if (!is_notes) {
          min_allowed_pos <- candidates[i] + 1L
        }
      } else {
        # Candidate is BEFORE previous article — re-search forward only
        pos2 <- find_in_window(text, arts$title[i], min_allowed_pos, body_end,
                               allow_s10=TRUE)
        if (!is.na(pos2)) {
          fwd_jump   <- pos2 - candidates[i]
          near_start <- candidates[i] <= (body_start + 5000L)
          # Guard against cascade: reject reorders with large jumps, or
          # reorders that land far from the expected page position.
          # This prevents cascading displacement where one bad reorder
          # pushes min_allowed_pos far forward, forcing all subsequent
          # articles to cluster at the end of the file.
          reject_reorder <- FALSE
          if ((near_start && fwd_jump > 50000L) || fwd_jump > 40000L) {
            reject_reorder <- TRUE
            cat(sprintf("    [%02d] rejected reorder: @%d -> @%d (jump %d too large)\n",
                        i, candidates[i], pos2, fwd_jump))
          } else if (fwd_jump > 15000L && nrow(page_index) >= 6 &&
                     arts$page[i] > 0) {
            # For moderate jumps (15K-40K), also check page-estimate distance.
            # If the reorder lands far from the page estimate, it's likely
            # a cascade artifact rather than a correct placement.
            pg_est_reorder <- page_to_pos(page_index, arts$page[i],
                                          body_start, body_end)
            if (!is.na(pg_est_reorder) &&
                abs(pos2 - pg_est_reorder) > 35000L) {
              reject_reorder <- TRUE
              cat(sprintf("    [%02d] rejected reorder: @%d -> @%d (page est @%d, dist %d too far)\n",
                          i, candidates[i], pos2, pg_est_reorder,
                          abs(pos2 - pg_est_reorder)))
            }
          }
          if (reject_reorder) {
            # Leave positions[i] = NA for Step 6 / Step 11 fallback
          } else {
            positions[i] <- max(snap_to_para_start(text, pos2, min_allowed_pos), min_allowed_pos)
            min_allowed_pos <- positions[i] + 1L
            cat(sprintf("    [%02d] reordered: was @%d, now @%d (forward-only)\n",
                        i, candidates[i], positions[i]))
          }
        }
        # If not found forward, leave as NA for Step 6 fallback
      }
    }
  }

  # ---- Report found positions ----
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(arts))) {
    pg <- arts$page[i]
    cat(sprintf("    [%02d] pg%3s %-48s -> %s\n", i,
                ifelse(is.numeric(pg), as.character(as.integer(pg)), pg),
                str_sub(arts$title[i],1,48),
                if (!is.na(positions[i])) paste0("@", positions[i]) else "MISS"))
  }

  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  # Step 6: Page-position fallback for remaining misses
  # Enhanced: also try to find a "By [Author]" pattern near the
  # estimated page position, or an ALL-CAPS heading, when the
  # garbled CONTENTS title itself can't be matched.
  # All positions are constrained to be:
  #   >= min_allowed_pos (previous article's position + 1)
  #   <  max_allowed_pos (next article's already-accepted position)
  # This maintains monotonic ordering from beginning to end of file.
  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  min_allowed_pos <- body_start
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(arts))) {
    if (!is.na(positions[i])) {
      min_allowed_pos <- positions[i] + 1L
      next
    }
    if (arts$page[i] <= 0L || nrow(page_index) < 3) next
    # Find the next already-accepted position (max constraint)
    max_allowed_pos <- body_end
    for (j in (i+1L):min(nrow(arts), i+20L)) {
      if (j > nrow(arts)) break
      if (!is.na(positions[j])) { max_allowed_pos <- positions[j] - 1L; break }
    }
    est <- page_to_pos(page_index, arts$page[i], body_start, body_end)
    # Clamp estimate to be within [min_allowed_pos, max_allowed_pos]
    est <- max(est, min_allowed_pos)
    est <- min(est, max_allowed_pos)
    if (est >= body_start && est <= max_allowed_pos) {
      # Search backward from est, but never before min_allowed_pos
      back_start <- max(min_allowed_pos, est - 2000L)
      search_end <- min(max_allowed_pos, est + 500L)
      pos_try <- find_in_window(text, arts$title[i], back_start, search_end,
                                allow_s10=TRUE)
      if (!is.na(pos_try) && pos_try >= min_allowed_pos && pos_try <= max_allowed_pos) {
        positions[i] <- max(snap_to_para_start(text, pos_try, min_allowed_pos), min_allowed_pos)
      } else {
        # Try extracting author last name from title and searching for "By [Name]"
        author_m_fb <- str_match(arts$title[i],
          "^.{2,40}?\\s+([A-Z][a-zA-Z.]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-zA-Z.]*){1,3})$")
        if (!is.na(author_m_fb[1,1])) {
          fb_author <- str_trim(author_m_fb[1,2])
          fb_last   <- str_extract(fb_author, "[A-Za-z]+$")
          if (!is.na(fb_last) && nchar(fb_last) >= 4) {
            fb_chunk <- str_sub(text, back_start, search_end)
            fb_pat   <- paste0("[Bb]y\\s+[A-Z][a-zA-Z.]+(?:\\s+[A-Z.]+){0,2}\\s+",
                               str_replace_all(fb_last, "([.?*+^$\\[\\]\\\\(){}|])", "\\\\\\1"),
                               "\\b")
            fb_m <- tryCatch(str_locate(fb_chunk, regex(fb_pat, ignore_case=FALSE)),
                             error=function(e) matrix(NA_integer_,1,2))
            if (!is.na(fb_m[1,1])) {
              fb_pos <- back_start + fb_m[1,1] - 1L
              if (fb_pos >= min_allowed_pos && fb_pos <= max_allowed_pos) {
                positions[i] <- max(snap_to_para_start(text, fb_pos, min_allowed_pos, max_back=300L),
                                    min_allowed_pos)
                cat(sprintf("    [%02d] pg%3d %-48s -> @%d [author-near-page]\n",
                            i, arts$page[i], str_sub(arts$title[i],1,48), positions[i]))
              }
            }
          }
        }
        if (!is.na(positions[i])) {
          min_allowed_pos <- positions[i] + 1L; next
        }
        # Text pattern inference
        fb_chunk2 <- str_sub(text, back_start, search_end)

        # Look for ALL-CAPS headings
        caps_pat <- "(?<=\\n)\\s*([A-Z][A-Z ]{5,60})\\s*(?=\\n)"
        caps_m   <- str_locate_all(fb_chunk2, regex(caps_pat))[[1]]
        if (nrow(caps_m) > 0) {
          for (ci in seq_len(nrow(caps_m))) {
            caps_text <- str_sub(fb_chunk2, caps_m[ci,1], caps_m[ci,2])
            if (!str_detect(caps_text, regex("RELIEF SOCIETY|MAGAZINE|ADVERTISERS",
                                             ignore_case=FALSE))) {
              fb_pos <- back_start + caps_m[ci,1] - 1L
              if (fb_pos >= min_allowed_pos && fb_pos <= max_allowed_pos) {
                positions[i] <- max(snap_to_para_start(text, fb_pos, min_allowed_pos, max_back=300L),
                                    min_allowed_pos)
                cat(sprintf("    [%02d] pg%3d %-48s -> @%d [caps-heading]\n",
                            i, arts$page[i], str_sub(arts$title[i],1,48), positions[i]))
                break
              }
            }
          }
          if (!is.na(positions[i])) {
            min_allowed_pos <- positions[i] + 1L; next
          }
        }

        # Look for "By [Name]" byline pattern
        byline_pat <- "(?<=\\n)\\s*[Bb]y\\s+[A-Z][a-zA-Z.]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-zA-Z.]+){1,3}\\s*(?=\\n)"
        byline_m   <- str_locate_all(fb_chunk2, regex(byline_pat))[[1]]
        if (nrow(byline_m) > 0) {
          fb_pos <- back_start + byline_m[1,1] - 1L
          if (fb_pos >= min_allowed_pos && fb_pos <= max_allowed_pos) {
            positions[i] <- max(snap_to_para_start(text, fb_pos, min_allowed_pos, max_back=300L),
                                min_allowed_pos)
            cat(sprintf("    [%02d] pg%3d %-48s -> @%d [byline-pattern]\n",
                        i, arts$page[i], str_sub(arts$title[i],1,48), positions[i]))
          }
        }
        if (!is.na(positions[i])) {
          min_allowed_pos <- positions[i] + 1L; next
        }

        # Final fallback: snap to paragraph break, constrained to valid range
        positions[i] <- min(
          max(snap_to_para_start(text, est, min_allowed_pos, max_back=1000L),
              min_allowed_pos),
          max_allowed_pos)
      }
      # Update min_allowed_pos after any assignment.
      # Use proportional advancement: advance by at least half the expected
      # page gap to the next article.  This prevents consecutive page-est
      # articles from clustering at sequential +1 offsets, which produces
      # tiny/empty files for articles on adjacent pages.
      if (!is.na(positions[i])) {
        next_pg <- NA_integer_
        for (j in (i + 1L):min(nrow(arts), i + 10L)) {
          if (j > nrow(arts)) break
          if (arts$page[j] > 0) { next_pg <- arts$page[j]; break }
        }
        if (!is.na(next_pg) && arts$page[i] > 0 && next_pg >= arts$page[i]) {
          pg_diff <- max(1L, next_pg - arts$page[i])
          min_gap <- as.integer(CPP * pg_diff / 3L)
          min_allowed_pos <- positions[i] + max(1L, min_gap)
        } else {
          min_allowed_pos <- positions[i] + 1L
        }
      }
      cat(sprintf("    [%02d] pg%3d %-48s -> @%d [page-est]\n",
                  i, arts$page[i], str_sub(arts$title[i],1,48), positions[i]))
    }
  }

  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  # Step 7: Assign boundaries
  # Each article: start_pos to (next_start - 1), last to body_end.
  # Missed articles (not found in OCR) get placeholder files.
  # ----------------------------------------------------------

  # Build a sorted table of found articles
  found_idx <- which(!is.na(positions))
  miss_idx  <- which(is.na(positions))

  saved_count <- 0L

  if (length(found_idx) == 0) {
    # Nothing found — save entire body as one Misc file
    write_file(str_sub(text, body_start, body_end),
               file.path(out_folder, paste0(prefix, "_Misc_fullbody.txt")))
    cat("  No article positions found. Full body saved.\n")
    return(data.frame(file=filename, n_contents=nrow(arts), n_saved=0L,
                      n_missed=nrow(arts), total_chars=len, stringsAsFactors=FALSE))
  }

  art_tbl <- data.frame(
    orig_i   = found_idx,
    position = positions[found_idx],
    stringsAsFactors=FALSE)
  # Positions are already in monotonic page order from Pass B enforcement.
  # Do NOT re-sort by position — this preserves the CONTENTS ordering
  # and prevents articles from being spliced out of order.

  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  # Step 7b: Group near-adjacent articles for merging.
  #
  # Articles listed separately in the CONTENTS sometimes correspond to
  # continuous text in the OCR (e.g. Morning + Afternoon sessions,
  # or three short companion pieces with no gap between them).
  # When consecutive articles are within MERGE_THRESH characters of
  # each other — including the exact-same-position case (both resolve
  # to body_start or to the same section heading) — they are merged
  # into ONE output file.  The file is labelled with the title of the
  # first article in the group; all subsequent titles are appended
  # after a " + " separator so the combined content is traceable.
  #
  # This replaces the old "+1 offset" disambiguation which created
  # 1-character stub files for all but the last article at a shared
  # position.
  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  MERGE_THRESH <- 10L

  groups    <- list()
  cur_grp   <- c(1L)
  for (k in seq_len(nrow(art_tbl))) {
    if (k == 1L) {
      cur_grp <- c(k)
    } else if (art_tbl$position[k] - art_tbl$position[k - 1L] <= MERGE_THRESH) {
      cur_grp <- c(cur_grp, k)
    } else {
      groups[[length(groups) + 1L]] <- cur_grp
      cur_grp <- c(k)
    }
  }
  groups[[length(groups) + 1L]] <- cur_grp
  n_groups <- length(groups)

  # Compute start / end position and list of orig_i for every merged group
  grp_start <- integer(n_groups)
  grp_end   <- integer(n_groups)
  grp_orig  <- vector("list", n_groups)
  for (g in seq_len(n_groups)) {
    rows          <- groups[[g]]
    grp_start[g]  <- art_tbl$position[rows[1L]]
    grp_end[g]    <- if (g < n_groups)
                       art_tbl$position[groups[[g + 1L]][1L]] - 1L
                     else
                       body_end
    grp_orig[[g]] <- art_tbl$orig_i[rows]
  }

  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  # Step 7c: Absorb tiny article groups into the next group.
  #
  # When position estimation places multiple CONTENTS entries on
  # adjacent pages (e.g. pages 149, 150, 151), the text can get
  # fragmented into tiny slivers — some just 20-30 characters.
  # These fragments are not usable standalone articles.
  #
  # Fix: if a group's text span is below TINY_THRESH characters,
  # absorb it into the NEXT group (extend the next group backward
  # to include the tiny group's text).  Iterate until no tiny
  # non-last groups remain.
  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  TINY_THRESH <- 250L

  repeat {
    tiny_found <- FALSE
    for (g in seq_len(length(grp_start) - 1L)) {
      span <- grp_end[g] - grp_start[g] + 1L
      if (span < TINY_THRESH) {
        cat(sprintf("    Tiny group [%s] (%d chars) absorbed into next group\n",
                    paste(grp_orig[[g]], collapse=","), span))
        # Extend the next group backward to include this group's text
        grp_start[g + 1L] <- grp_start[g]
        grp_orig[[g + 1L]] <- c(grp_orig[[g]], grp_orig[[g + 1L]])
        # Remove this group
        grp_start <- grp_start[-(g)]
        grp_end   <- grp_end[-(g)]
        grp_orig  <- grp_orig[-(g)]
        tiny_found <- TRUE
        break   # restart scan since indices shifted
      }
    }
    if (!tiny_found) break
  }
  n_groups <- length(grp_start)

  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  # Step 8: FRONT MATTER
  # Everything from body_start up to the first group's start.
  #
  # When few page markers exist, the first found article may be
  # positioned far into the file (via [page-est]) while the real
  # article text starts at body_start.  A large front_matter gap
  # (>5000 chars) almost certainly contains article content that
  # was mismatched to later positions.  In that case, extend the
  # first article group backward to body_start so the text stays
  # with the article files instead of being spliced into Misc.
  # Small gaps (<= 5000 chars) are genuine front matter (masthead,
  # frontispiece caption, etc.) and are saved as Misc as before.
  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  if (grp_start[1L] > body_start) {
    front <- clean_headers(str_sub(text, body_start, grp_start[1L] - 1L))
    if (nchar(board_text) > 50) front <- str_replace(front, fixed(board_text), "")
    front <- str_trim(front)
    if (nchar(front) > 5000L) {
      # Large gap — likely contains article text.  Extend the first
      # article group to start at body_start so nothing is spliced off.
      grp_start[1L] <- body_start
      cat(sprintf("  NOTE: Large front gap (%s chars) — prepended to first article group\n",
                  format(nchar(front), big.mark=",")))
    } else if (nchar(front) > 0) {
      write_file(front,
                 file.path(out_folder, paste0(prefix, "_Misc_front_matter.txt")))
      cat(sprintf("  Misc: front_matter saved (%s chars)\n",
                  format(nchar(front), big.mark=",")))
    }
  }

  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  # Step 9: SAVE EACH (MERGED) ARTICLE GROUP and GAPS between them
  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  for (g in seq_len(n_groups)) {
    orig_is  <- grp_orig[[g]]
    a_start  <- grp_start[g]
    a_end    <- grp_end[g]

    art_raw  <- str_sub(text, a_start, a_end)
    art_text <- clean_headers(art_raw)
    if (nchar(board_text) > 50)
      art_text <- str_replace(art_text, fixed(board_text), "")
    art_text <- str_trim(art_text)

    # --- Strip leading continuation/conclusion markers ---
    # When position estimation draws the article boundary slightly before
    # the actual title, the tail of the PREVIOUS article leaks in.
    # This tail typically ends with "(Continued on page NNN)".
    # Example: "Their untiring efforts and (Continued on page 221) PAGE 161
    #           The Promise Of The Gospel..."
    # Strip EVERYTHING up to and including the continuation marker if it
    # appears within the first 500 characters of the article text.
    if (nchar(art_text) > 100L) {
      leading_chunk <- str_sub(art_text, 1L, min(500L, nchar(art_text)))
      cont_lead_m <- str_locate(leading_chunk,
                      regex("\\(?(?:Continued|Concluded)\\s+(?:on|from)\\s+page\\s+\\d+\\)?",
                            ignore_case = TRUE))
      if (!is.na(cont_lead_m[1,1])) {
        stripped_lead <- str_trim(str_sub(art_text, 1L, cont_lead_m[1,2]))
        art_text <- str_trim(str_sub(art_text, cont_lead_m[1,2] + 1L, nchar(art_text)))
        if (nchar(stripped_lead) > 5L) {
          cat(sprintf("    Leading continuation stripped from [%02d]: '%s' (%d chars)\n",
                      g, str_sub(stripped_lead, 1, 60), nchar(stripped_lead)))
        }
      }
    }

    # --- Truncate at "(Continued/Concluded from page NNN)" from OTHER articles ---
    # In the physical magazine, articles are sometimes interrupted and
    # continued on a later page.  The continuation text is then placed
    # after an unrelated article in the OCR stream.  When we extract an
    # article's block of text, the continuation from the OTHER article
    # gets spliced onto the end.
    #
    # Detection: search for "(Continued from page NNN)" or
    # "(Concluded from page NNN)" AFTER the first 500 characters.
    # Articles that ARE themselves continuations will have the marker
    # very near the start (within ~200 chars) and are left alone.
    if (nchar(art_text) > 600L) {
      cont_from_m <- str_locate_all(art_text,
                       regex("\\(?(?:Continued|Concluded) from page \\d+\\)?",
                             ignore_case = FALSE))[[1]]
      if (nrow(cont_from_m) > 0) {
        # Find the first occurrence after position 500
        late_hits <- which(cont_from_m[, 1] > 500L)
        if (length(late_hits) > 0) {
          hit_start <- cont_from_m[late_hits[1], 1]
          # Search backward up to 500 chars for a paragraph break or line break
          lookback_start <- max(1L, hit_start - 500L)
          lookback_text  <- str_sub(art_text, lookback_start, hit_start - 1L)

          # Prefer paragraph break (double newline) for a clean cut
          para_breaks <- str_locate_all(lookback_text, "\n\\s*\n")[[1]]
          if (nrow(para_breaks) > 0) {
            # Character before the first \n of the paragraph break
            cut_pos <- lookback_start + para_breaks[nrow(para_breaks), 1] - 2L
          } else {
            # Fall back to single newline
            newlines <- str_locate_all(lookback_text, "\n")[[1]]
            if (nrow(newlines) > 0) {
              cut_pos <- lookback_start + newlines[nrow(newlines), 1] - 2L
            } else {
              cut_pos <- hit_start - 1L
            }
          }

          if (cut_pos > 100L && cut_pos < nchar(art_text) - 50L) {
            stripped_cont <- str_trim(str_sub(art_text, cut_pos + 1L, nchar(art_text)))
            art_text      <- str_trim(str_sub(art_text, 1L, cut_pos))
            if (nchar(stripped_cont) > 5L) {
              cont_fn <- file.path(out_folder,
                                   paste0(prefix, "_Misc_cont_", sprintf("%02d", g), ".txt"))
              write_file(stripped_cont, cont_fn)
              cat(sprintf("    Continuation from other article stripped from [%02d]: %s chars -> Misc_cont_%02d\n",
                          g, format(nchar(stripped_cont), big.mark = ","), g))
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }

    # --- Truncate at "(Continued/Concluded on page NNN)" ---
    # When the article text ends with "(Continued on page 221)" followed
    # by content from the NEXT article (which starts immediately after
    # in the OCR stream), cut AFTER the marker — keep the marker as
    # part of this article, discard everything after it.
    if (nchar(art_text) > 600L) {
      cont_on_m <- str_locate_all(art_text,
                     regex("\\(?(?:Continued|Concluded)\\s+on\\s+page\\s+\\d+\\)?",
                           ignore_case = FALSE))[[1]]
      if (nrow(cont_on_m) > 0) {
        late_on <- which(cont_on_m[, 1] > 500L)
        if (length(late_on) > 0) {
          cut_after <- cont_on_m[late_on[1], 2]
          remaining <- nchar(art_text) - cut_after
          if (remaining > 100L) {
            stripped_on <- str_trim(str_sub(art_text, cut_after + 1L, nchar(art_text)))
            art_text    <- str_trim(str_sub(art_text, 1L, cut_after))
            if (nchar(stripped_on) > 5L) {
              cont_fn <- file.path(out_folder,
                                   paste0(prefix, "_Misc_cont_", sprintf("%02d", g), ".txt"))
              # Append if file already exists from "from page" truncation
              if (file.exists(cont_fn)) {
                existing <- read_file(cont_fn)
                write_file(paste0(existing, "\n\n", stripped_on), cont_fn)
              } else {
                write_file(stripped_on, cont_fn)
              }
              cat(sprintf("    Trailing '(Continued on page)' stripped from [%02d]: %s chars\n",
                          g, format(nchar(stripped_on), big.mark = ",")))
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }

    # --- Strip trailing ads from the LAST article group ---
    # The last group extends to body_end (== file end for Vol24-26), so
    # back-cover ads (Beneficial Life, Deseret News Press, etc.) are
    # swallowed into the last article's text.  Find the EARLIEST ad
    # marker across a broad list, then cut at the preceding paragraph
    # break to cleanly separate article from ads.
    if (g == n_groups) {
      ad_markers <- c(
        # Specific ad copy / taglines (appear early in ad section)
        "Invest\\s+Your\\s+Time\\s+in\\s+Business\\s+Education",
        "Bind\\s+Your\\s+Relief\\s+Society\\s+Magazine",
        "Preserve\\s+in\\s+bound\\s+volumes",
        "FOUR\\s+WAYS\\s+BETTER",
        "L\\.?\\s*D\\.?\\s*S\\.?\\s+TRAINING\\s+PAYS",
        "Planning\\s+Your\\s+Future.*will\\s+explain",
        # Advertiser names with addresses (clearly ads, not article mentions)
        "DESERET\\s+BOOK\\s+COMPANY\\s+\\d+\\s+East",
        "DESERET\\s+NEWS\\s+PRESS\\s+\\d+\\s+Richards",
        "L\\.?\\s*D\\.?\\s*S\\.?\\s+BUSINESS\\s+COLLEGE\\s+\\d+\\s+North",
        "KNIGHT.?S?\\s+SPRING\\s+CANYON\\s+COAL",
        "BLUE\\s+BLAZE\\s+COAL",
        "ARDEN\\s+DAIRY",
        "BENNETT\\s+GLASS\\s+&?\\s*PAINT",
        "FAIRBANKS.MORSE",
        "MOUNTAIN\\s+FUEL\\s+SUPPLY",
        "UTAH\\s+POWER\\s+&\\s+LIGHT",
        "SALT\\s+LAKE\\s+GLASS\\s+&\\s+PAINT",
        "SUGAR\\s+HOUSE\\s+LUMBER",
        "CRYSTAL\\s+PALACE\\s+MARKET",
        "PEERLESS\\s+LAUNDRY",
        "INTERMOUNTAIN\\s+BEET\\s+SUGAR",
        "LOCAL\\s+BEET\\s+SUGAR\\s+FOR",
        "Z\\.?\\s*C\\.?\\s*M\\.?\\s*I\\.?\\s+Clothing\\s+Factory",
        "Personalized\\s+Christmas\\s+Cards",
        "DESERET\\s+MORTUARY",
        # Ad footers and boilerplate
        "When\\s+Buying\\s+Mention\\s+Relief\\s+Society",
        "Mention\\s+Relief\\s+Society\\s+Magazine",
        "THE\\s+RELIEF\\s+SOCIETY\\s+MAGAZINE\\s+MORE\\s+THAN\\s+\\d",
        # Insurance (usually last)
        "BENEFICIAL\\s+LIFE\\s+INSURANCE",
        "BENEFICIAL\\s+LIFE\\s+(?:BLDG|BUILDING)",
        "HOME\\s+OFFICE[^\\n]*SALT\\s+LAKE\\s+CITY"
      )
      art_len <- nchar(art_text)
      # Search the last 50% to catch early ads
      search_from <- as.integer(art_len * 0.50)
      tail_text   <- str_sub(art_text, search_from, art_len)

      # Find the EARLIEST ad marker across ALL patterns
      earliest_pos <- NA_integer_
      for (amp in ad_markers) {
        am <- str_locate(tail_text, regex(amp, ignore_case = TRUE))
        if (!is.na(am[1, 1])) {
          abs_pos <- search_from + am[1, 1] - 1L
          if (is.na(earliest_pos) || abs_pos < earliest_pos)
            earliest_pos <- abs_pos
        }
      }

      if (!is.na(earliest_pos) && earliest_pos > 100L) {
        # Search backward from the ad marker to find a paragraph break
        # (double-newline) or start-of-line, for a clean cut.
        lookback <- str_sub(art_text,
                            max(1L, earliest_pos - 500L), earliest_pos)
        para_break <- str_locate_all(lookback, "\n\\s*\n")[[1]]
        if (nrow(para_break) > 0) {
          # Cut at the LAST paragraph break before the ad marker
          ad_cut <- max(1L, earliest_pos - 500L) + para_break[nrow(para_break), 2]
        } else {
          # No paragraph break found — cut right before the marker
          ad_cut <- earliest_pos - 1L
        }

        if (ad_cut > 100L) {
          stripped_ads <- str_trim(str_sub(art_text, ad_cut + 1L, art_len))
          art_text     <- str_trim(str_sub(art_text, 1L, ad_cut))
          if (nchar(stripped_ads) > 5L) {
            back_fn <- file.path(out_folder,
                                 paste0(prefix, "_Misc_back_matter.txt"))
            write_file(stripped_ads, back_fn)
            cat(sprintf("  Trailing ads stripped from last article: %s chars -> back_matter\n",
                        format(nchar(stripped_ads), big.mark = ",")))
          }
        }
      }
    }

    # Combined title: first article's title (+ subsequent if merged)
    primary_i     <- orig_is[1L]
    combined_title <- paste(arts$title[orig_is], collapse = " + ")
    display_title  <- str_sub(combined_title, 1, 60)

    saved_count <- saved_count + length(orig_is)   # count articles covered, not files
    fn <- file.path(out_folder,
      paste0(prefix, "_", sprintf("%02d", g), "_",
             arts$category[primary_i], "_",
             short_title_filename(arts$title[primary_i]), ".txt"))
    write_file(art_text, fn)

    if (length(orig_is) > 1L) {
      cat(sprintf("    Saved [%02d] %-60s %s chars  [MERGED %d articles]\n",
                  g, display_title, format(nchar(art_text), big.mark=","),
                  length(orig_is)))
    } else {
      cat(sprintf("    Saved [%02d] %-48s %s chars\n",
                  g, str_sub(arts$title[primary_i], 1, 48),
                  format(nchar(art_text), big.mark=",")))
    }

    # Gap to next group — save even small gaps so NO text is ever lost
    if (g < n_groups) {
      g_start <- grp_end[g] + 1L
      g_end   <- grp_start[g + 1L] - 1L
      if (g_end >= g_start) {
        gap <- str_trim(clean_headers(str_sub(text, g_start, g_end)))
        if (nchar(board_text) > 50) gap <- str_replace(gap, fixed(board_text), "")
        gap <- str_trim(gap)
        if (nchar(gap) > 5L) {
          gfn <- file.path(out_folder,
            paste0(prefix, "_Misc_gap_", sprintf("%02d", g), "_",
                   sprintf("%02d", g + 1L), ".txt"))
          write_file(gap, gfn)
          cat(sprintf("    Misc gap [%02d]->[%02d]: %s chars\n",
                      g, g + 1L, format(nchar(gap), big.mark=",")))
        }
      }
    }
  }

  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  # Step 10: BACK MATTER
  # Everything from the last group's end to body_end.
  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  last_end <- grp_end[n_groups]
  if (last_end < body_end) {
    back <- str_trim(clean_headers(str_sub(text, last_end + 1L, body_end)))
    if (nchar(back) > 5L) {
      write_file(back,
                 file.path(out_folder, paste0(prefix, "_Misc_back_matter.txt")))
      cat(sprintf("  Misc: back_matter saved (%s chars)\n",
                  format(nchar(back), big.mark=",")))
    }
  }

  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  # Step 11: Pre-body search for MISS articles, then placeholders
  # Some CONTENTS entries refer to text in the pre-body region.
  # If found, extract them as separate article files (not just Misc).
  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  true_miss_idx <- integer(0)
  prebody_article_num <- 0L
  if (length(miss_idx) > 0) {
    for (mi in miss_idx) {
      # Search pre-body regions EXCLUDING the CONTENTS listing.
      # Region 1: before CONTENTS (e.g. cover page text, early poems)
      # Region 2: between CONTENTS end and body start (e.g. frontispiece, ads)
      # This prevents CONTENTS entries from being extracted as bogus articles.
      pb_pos <- NA_integer_
      ct_s <- if (!is.na(s$contents_start)) s$contents_start else 1L
      ct_e <- if (!is.na(s$contents_end))   s$contents_end   else 0L
      if (ct_s > 1L) {
        pb_pos <- find_in_window(text, arts$title[mi], 1L, ct_s - 1L,
                                 allow_s10=TRUE)
      }
      if (is.na(pb_pos) && ct_e + 1L < body_start) {
        pb_pos <- find_in_window(text, arts$title[mi], ct_e + 1L,
                                 body_start - 1L, allow_s10=TRUE)
      }
      if (!is.na(pb_pos)) {
        # Found in pre-body — extract as separate article file
        # Find extent: from title position to next double-newline or body_start
        pb_region <- str_sub(text, pb_pos, body_start - 1L)
        # Find first double-newline after initial content (skip first 20 chars)
        end_break <- str_locate(str_sub(pb_region, 20L, nchar(pb_region)),
                                "\n\\s*\n")
        if (!is.na(end_break[1,1])) {
          # Check if there's meaningful text after the break; if < 200 chars
          # of remaining content, take it all (it's likely part of the article)
          remaining <- nchar(pb_region) - (20L + end_break[1,2])
          if (remaining > 200L) {
            pb_text <- str_trim(str_sub(pb_region, 1L, 20L + end_break[1,2]))
          } else {
            pb_text <- str_trim(pb_region)
          }
        } else {
          pb_text <- str_trim(pb_region)
        }
        # Only save if the text is substantial (not just a masthead line)
        if (nchar(pb_text) > 30L && !str_detect(arts$title[mi],
            regex("PUBLISHED\\s+MONTHLY|Bishop.s\\s+Building", ignore_case=TRUE))) {
          prebody_article_num <- prebody_article_num + 1L
          pb_fn <- file.path(out_folder,
            paste0(prefix, "_00pb", sprintf("%d", prebody_article_num), "_",
                   arts$category[mi], "_",
                   short_title_filename(arts$title[mi]), ".txt"))
          write_file(pb_text, pb_fn)
          saved_count <- saved_count + 1L
          cat(sprintf("    Saved pre-body article: '%s' (%s chars)\n",
                      str_sub(arts$title[mi],1,50),
                      format(nchar(pb_text), big.mark=",")))
        } else {
          # Masthead or tiny text — already in Misc, just count
          saved_count <- saved_count + 1L
          cat(sprintf("    Captured in _00_Misc: '%s' (pre-body @%d)\n",
                      str_sub(arts$title[mi],1,50), pb_pos))
        }
      } else {
        true_miss_idx <- c(true_miss_idx, mi)
      }
    }
  }

  if (length(true_miss_idx) > 0) {
    for (mi in true_miss_idx) {
      # Log to console only — no placeholder files.  The text for these
      # articles is genuinely absent from the OCR body (Frontispiece pages,
      # poems/pieces on un-scanned pages, or garbled OCR titles).
      cat(sprintf("    NOT IN SOURCE: '%s' (pg %s)\n",
                  str_sub(arts$title[mi],1,50), arts$page[mi]))
    }
  }

  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  # Step 11b: Extract pre-body articles misplaced at body_start.
  # When page-est clusters multiple articles at/near body_start,
  # their actual text is often in the post-CONTENTS pre-body region
  # (poems, cover notes, frontispiece text).  Search there and
  # extract as separate _00pb files.
  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  bodystart_idx <- which(!is.na(positions) &
                         positions >= body_start &
                         positions <= body_start + 50L)
  if (length(bodystart_idx) >= 2L) {
    ct_e <- if (!is.na(s$contents_end)) s$contents_end else 0L
    if (ct_e + 1L < body_start - 100L) {
      pb_found <- data.frame(idx=integer(0), pos=integer(0),
                             stringsAsFactors=FALSE)
      for (bi in bodystart_idx) {
        pb_pos <- find_in_window(text, arts$title[bi], ct_e + 1L,
                                 body_start - 1L, allow_s10=TRUE)
        if (!is.na(pb_pos)) {
          pb_found <- rbind(pb_found, data.frame(idx=bi, pos=pb_pos))
        }
      }
      if (nrow(pb_found) > 0L) {
        pb_found <- pb_found[order(pb_found$pos), ]
        # Deduplicate: if two matches are within 100 chars, keep earliest
        if (nrow(pb_found) > 1L) {
          keep <- rep(TRUE, nrow(pb_found))
          for (k in 2:nrow(pb_found)) {
            if (pb_found$pos[k] - pb_found$pos[k-1] < 100L) keep[k] <- FALSE
          }
          pb_found <- pb_found[keep, , drop=FALSE]
        }
        for (k in seq_len(nrow(pb_found))) {
          start_pos <- pb_found$pos[k]
          end_pos   <- if (k < nrow(pb_found)) pb_found$pos[k+1] - 1L
                       else body_start - 1L
          pb_text <- str_trim(str_sub(text, start_pos, end_pos))
          # Strip trailing Vol./No. masthead line
          pb_text <- str_replace(pb_text,
            regex("\\s*Vol\\.?\\s*\\d+\\s+[A-Z]+,?\\s+\\d{4}\\s+No\\.?\\s*\\d+\\s*$",
                  ignore_case=TRUE), "")
          pb_text <- str_trim(pb_text)
          ai <- pb_found$idx[k]
          if (nchar(pb_text) > 50L &&
              !str_detect(pb_text, regex("^\\s*PUBLISHED\\s+MONTHLY",
                                        ignore_case=TRUE))) {
            prebody_article_num <- prebody_article_num + 1L
            pb_fn <- file.path(out_folder,
              paste0(prefix, "_00pb", sprintf("%d", prebody_article_num), "_",
                     arts$category[ai], "_",
                     short_title_filename(arts$title[ai]), ".txt"))
            write_file(pb_text, pb_fn)
            saved_count <- saved_count + 1L
            cat(sprintf("    Saved pre-body [body_start] article: '%s' (%s chars)\n",
                        str_sub(arts$title[ai],1,50),
                        format(nchar(pb_text), big.mark=",")))
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }

  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  # Step 12: Scan Misc_cont files for embedded CONTENTS articles.
  # When Fix 14/17b strips continuation text from an article group,
  # sometimes a following article's body gets swept into the
  # Misc_cont file.  Find these by searching for the first
  # distinctive word of each CONTENTS title after a paragraph break.
  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  misc_cont_files <- sort(list.files(out_folder,
                           pattern="_Misc_cont_\\d+\\.txt$",
                           full.names=TRUE))
  if (length(misc_cont_files) > 0L) {
    common_words <- c("the", "this", "that", "with", "from", "their",
                      "about", "what", "which", "they", "them",
                      "chapter", "part", "page", "society", "article",
                      "relief", "magazine", "notes", "field", "editorial",
                      "general", "lesson", "work")
    for (mcf in misc_cont_files) {
      mc_text <- read_file(mcf)
      if (nchar(mc_text) < 200L) next
      best_match <- NULL
      for (i in seq_len(nrow(arts))) {
        tw <- str_split(arts$title[i], "\\s+")[[1]]
        tw <- tw[nchar(tw) > 3L]
        tw <- tw[!tolower(tw) %in% common_words]
        if (length(tw) == 0L) next
        search_word <- tw[1]
        # Look for the distinctive word after a paragraph break (\n\n)
        # and at least 50 chars into the file (skip continuation header)
        m_all <- str_locate_all(mc_text,
                   regex(paste0("\\n\\s*\\n[^\\n]*?\\b", search_word, "\\b"),
                         ignore_case = TRUE))[[1]]
        if (nrow(m_all) > 0L) {
          for (r in seq_len(nrow(m_all))) {
            if (m_all[r,1] > 50L) {
              if (is.null(best_match) || m_all[r,1] < best_match$pos) {
                best_match <- list(idx=i, pos=m_all[r,1], word=search_word)
              }
              break
            }
          }
        }
      }
      if (!is.null(best_match)) {
        # Advance past the \n\n whitespace to the actual article line start
        split_pos <- best_match$pos
        while (split_pos <= nchar(mc_text) &&
               str_sub(mc_text, split_pos, split_pos) %in% c("\n", "\r", " ", "\t")) {
          split_pos <- split_pos + 1L
        }
        pre_text <- str_trim(str_sub(mc_text, 1L, split_pos - 1L))
        art_text_mc <- str_trim(str_sub(mc_text, split_pos, nchar(mc_text)))
        if (nchar(art_text_mc) > 50L) {
          ai <- best_match$idx
          prebody_article_num <- prebody_article_num + 1L
          art_fn <- file.path(out_folder,
            paste0(prefix, "_00pb", sprintf("%d", prebody_article_num), "_",
                   arts$category[ai], "_",
                   short_title_filename(arts$title[ai]), ".txt"))
          write_file(art_text_mc, art_fn)
          saved_count <- saved_count + 1L
          # Rewrite Misc_cont with only the continuation part
          if (nchar(pre_text) > 5L) {
            write_file(pre_text, mcf)
          } else {
            file.remove(mcf)
          }
          cat(sprintf("    Extracted from Misc_cont: '%s' (%s chars) [matched '%s']\n",
                      str_sub(arts$title[ai],1,50),
                      format(nchar(art_text_mc), big.mark=","),
                      best_match$word))
        }
      }
    }
  }

  n_missed <- length(true_miss_idx)
  cat(sprintf("\n  RESULT: %d saved, %d NOT IN SOURCE, %d total in CONTENTS\n",
              saved_count, n_missed, nrow(arts)))

  data.frame(file=filename, n_contents=nrow(arts), n_saved=saved_count,
             n_missed=n_missed, total_chars=len, stringsAsFactors=FALSE)
}
```

```{r run, message=FALSE}
# ============================================================
# MAIN LOOP
# ============================================================
cat("Relief Society Magazine — Universal Article Extractor\n")
cat(rep("=",70), "\n\n", sep="")

all_results <- list()

for (input_dir in INPUT_DIRS) {
  if (!dir.exists(input_dir)) {
    cat("WARNING: not found:", input_dir, "\n"); next
  }
  files <- sort(list.files(input_dir, pattern="\\.txt$",
                           full.names=TRUE, recursive=FALSE))
  cat("\n", rep("#",70), "\n", sep="")
  cat("FOLDER:", basename(input_dir), " — ", length(files), "file(s)\n")
  cat(rep("#",70), "\n", sep="")

  for (f in files) {
    res <- tryCatch(
      process_issue(f, OUTPUT_DIR),
      error=function(e) {
        cat("ERROR:", basename(f), "—", conditionMessage(e), "\n")
        data.frame(file=basename(f), n_contents=0L, n_saved=0L,
                   n_missed=0L, total_chars=0L, stringsAsFactors=FALSE)
      })
    all_results[[length(all_results)+1]] <- res
  }
}

results_df <- if (length(all_results)>0) do.call(rbind, all_results) else NULL
```

## Summary {.tabset}

### Results Table

```{r summary}
if (!is.null(results_df) && nrow(results_df)>0) {
  cat(sprintf("Files processed:       %d\n",   nrow(results_df)))
  cat(sprintf("Articles in CONTENTS:  %d\n",   sum(results_df$n_contents)))
  cat(sprintf("Articles saved:        %d\n",   sum(results_df$n_saved)))
  cat(sprintf("Not in source (OCR):   %d\n",   sum(results_df$n_missed)))
  cat(sprintf("Hit rate:              %.1f%%\n",
              sum(results_df$n_saved) / max(1, sum(results_df$n_contents)) * 100))
  cat("Output folder:", OUTPUT_DIR, "\n\n")

  kable(results_df,
        col.names=c("File","In CONTENTS","Saved","Not in Source","Total Chars"),
        format.args=list(big.mark=",")) %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options=c("striped","hover","condensed"),
                  full_width=FALSE) %>%
    column_spec(4, color=ifelse(results_df$n_missed > 0, "red", "darkgreen"))
}
```

### Notes on "Not In Source" Files

When an article listed in the CONTENTS cannot be found anywhere in the body text, the script saves a `_NOT_IN_SOURCE_` placeholder file. This happens when the original print pages were not included in the digitisation/OCR — the text simply is not in the source file. These are **not extraction errors**; they reflect genuine gaps in the OCR source material.

Gaps between articles are saved as `_Misc_gap_` files so that no text is ever discarded. The entire range from `body_start` to `body_end` is always fully covered.
